Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses [number of ounces per week] ounces per week. A leap year has 366 days, so she washes her hair [number of times per year] times in a leap year. Multiplying the number of times she uses shampoo by the amount used each time gives the total ounces needed for the year. Since each bottle contains 14 ounces, dividing the total ounces by 14 will determine how many bottles she needs to buy.

To calculate the exact number of bottles, first determine the total number of shampoo uses in a leap year by dividing 366 by 2, which equals [number of uses]. Then, multiply that by 1/4 to find the total ounces needed, resulting in [total ounces needed]. Dividing that by 14 gives the number of bottles Brittany must purchase to last through the entire leap year. This calculation ensures she has enough shampoo without running out.
